title_en: Eschatology and the Intercession of the Prophet
content_en:
Usul al-Sunnah devotes considerable attention to the realities of the unseen world that await humanity after death. Ahmad ibn Hanbal affirms the punishment and blessing of the grave as established realities. The questioning of the dead by the two angels Munkar and Nakir, the expansion of the grave for the righteous and its constriction for the wicked, and the experience of either preliminary reward or preliminary punishment before the Day of Resurrection: all of these are affirmed on the basis of authentic hadith reports. Ahmad did not allow rationalist objections to the physical plausibility of these events to diminish his confidence in them. If the Prophet reported them, they are true, and the believer affirms them without seeking to explain away their apparent implications.

The resurrection of the bodies is treated as a certainty. Allah will recreate the bodies of all who have died and reunite them with their souls for the final reckoning. The gathering (hashr) will take place on a vast plain, and every soul will be brought to account for what it did during its lifetime. The books of deeds will be distributed, and each person will read their own record. The mizan, the scale on which deeds are weighed, is affirmed as a literal physical instrument of divine justice. Ahmad accepts the apparent meaning of the Quranic and hadith descriptions of these events and rejects attempts to interpret them as metaphors for purely spiritual processes.

The Hawd, the Pool of the Prophet Muhammad, is among the articles that Usul al-Sunnah explicitly affirms. The Prophet described a vast pool that will be granted to him, from which his community will drink before the final reckoning, and drinking from it will bring permanent satisfaction. Ahmad accepts this description as literal truth. He similarly affirms the Sirat, the bridge stretched over Hellfire, and the reality of Paradise and Hellfire as existing places prepared for their respective inhabitants. The dwellers of Paradise will enjoy endless bliss, and the greatest of their joys will be the vision of Allah's face, which Ahmad affirms against the Mu'tazilite denial of the beatific vision.

The intercession (shafa'a) of the Prophet is one of the most prominent articles in Usul al-Sunnah. Ahmad affirms that the Prophet will intercede for the sinners of his community, that this intercession will be accepted by Allah, and that people who deserved Hell on account of their sins will be brought out of it through the Prophet's intercession and through divine mercy. This doctrine offers profound hope to Muslims who are conscious of their own shortcomings and who fear divine justice. Ahmad also affirms that other prophets, scholars, and martyrs will intercede for those they are permitted to intercede for. The Mu'tazilites rejected intercession for sinners because they held that divine justice required sinners to bear their full punishment, but Ahmad regarded this as a misunderstanding of divine mercy and a contradiction of explicit prophetic teaching.
content_id:
Ushul al-Sunnah mendedikasikan perhatian yang cukup besar pada realitas-realitas alam gaib yang menanti umat manusia setelah kematian. Ahmad ibn Hanbal menegaskan azab dan nikmat kubur sebagai realitas-realitas yang mapan. Pertanyaan-pertanyaan kepada orang yang meninggal oleh dua malaikat Munkar dan Nakir, perluasan kubur bagi orang-orang saleh dan penyempitannya bagi orang-orang jahat, serta pengalaman entah pahala pendahuluan atau hukuman pendahuluan sebelum Hari Kebangkitan: semua ini ditegaskan berdasarkan riwayat-riwayat hadis yang sahih. Ahmad tidak mengizinkan keberatan-keberatan rasionalis terhadap kemungkinan fisik dari peristiwa-peristiwa ini untuk mengurangi keyakinannya pada mereka. Jika Nabi melaporkannya, maka itu benar, dan orang beriman menegaskannya tanpa berusaha menjelaskan implikasi-implikasi yang tampak.

Kebangkitan jasad diperlakukan sebagai kepastian. Allah akan menciptakan kembali jasad semua orang yang telah meninggal dan menyatukannya kembali dengan ruh-ruh mereka untuk penghisaban akhir. Pengumpulan (hasyr) akan berlangsung di padang yang luas, dan setiap jiwa akan dimintai pertanggungjawaban atas apa yang dilakukannya selama hidupnya. Kitab-kitab amal perbuatan akan dibagikan, dan setiap orang akan membaca catatannya sendiri. Mizan, timbangan yang digunakan untuk menimbang amal perbuatan, ditegaskan sebagai instrumen keadilan ilahi yang secara harfiah fisik. Ahmad menerima makna zahir dari deskripsi-deskripsi Al-Quran dan hadis tentang peristiwa-peristiwa ini dan menolak upaya-upaya untuk menafsirkannya sebagai metafora untuk proses-proses spiritual semata.

Haudh, telaga Nabi Muhammad, adalah di antara pasal-pasal yang secara eksplisit ditegaskan oleh Ushul al-Sunnah. Nabi menggambarkan sebuah telaga yang luas yang akan diberikan kepadanya, dari mana umatnya akan minum sebelum penghisaban akhir, dan meminum darinya akan membawa kepuasan yang abadi. Ahmad menerima deskripsi ini sebagai kebenaran harfiah. Ia juga menegaskan Shirat, jembatan yang terbentang di atas Neraka, serta realitas Surga dan Neraka sebagai tempat-tempat yang ada yang dipersiapkan bagi masing-masing penghuninya. Para penghuni Surga akan menikmati kebahagiaan yang tak berakhir, dan kesenangan terbesar mereka adalah melihat wajah Allah, yang Ahmad tegaskan bertentangan dengan penolakan Mu'tazilah atas visi kebahagiaan ilahi.

Syafaat Nabi adalah salah satu pasal yang paling menonjol dalam Ushul al-Sunnah. Ahmad menegaskan bahwa Nabi akan memberikan syafaat bagi para pendosa dari umatnya, bahwa syafaat ini akan diterima oleh Allah, dan bahwa orang-orang yang berhak masuk Neraka karena dosa-dosa mereka akan dikeluarkan darinya melalui syafaat Nabi dan melalui rahmat ilahi. Doktrin ini memberikan harapan yang mendalam bagi kaum Muslim yang menyadari kekurangan-kekurangan mereka sendiri dan yang takut akan keadilan ilahi. Ahmad juga menegaskan bahwa para nabi lain, ulama, dan para syuhada akan memberikan syafaat bagi mereka yang diizinkan untuk mendapatkan syafaat mereka. Kaum Mu'tazilah menolak syafaat bagi para pendosa karena mereka berpendapat bahwa keadilan ilahi mengharuskan para pendosa menanggung hukuman penuh mereka, tetapi Ahmad menganggap ini sebagai kesalahpahaman tentang rahmat ilahi dan bertentangan dengan ajaran nabawi yang eksplisit.
content_ar: الأصول al-السنة devotes considerable attention to the realities of the unseen الدنيا that await humanity بعد الموت. أحمد ابن حنبل affirms the العقاب and البركة of the القبر as established realities. The questioning of the dead by the two الملائكة Munkar and Nakir, the expansion of the القبر for the الصالح and its constriction for the wicked, and the experience of either preliminary الأجر or preliminary العقاب قبل the Day of البعث: all of these are affirmed on the basis of authentic الحديث reports. أحمد did not allow rationalist objections to the physical plausibility of these events to diminish his confidence in them. إذا النبي ﷺ reported them, they are true, and the المؤمن affirms them without seeking to explain away their apparent implications. The البعث of the bodies is treated as a certainty. الله will recreate the bodies of all who have died and reunite them with their النفوس for the final reckoning. The gathering (hashr) will take place on a vast plain, and every النفس will be brought to account for what it did خلال its lifetime. The الكتب of deeds will be distributed, and each person will read their own record. The الميزان, the scale on which deeds are weighed, is affirmed as a literal physical instrument of divine العدل. أحمد accepts the apparent meaning of the القرآني and الحديث descriptions of these events and rejects attempts to interpret them as metaphors for purely spiritual processes. The Hawd, the Pool of النبي ﷺ محمد ﷺ, is among the articles that الأصول al-السنة explicitly affirms. النبي ﷺ described a vast pool that will be granted to him, from which his الجماعة will drink قبل the final reckoning, and drinking from it will bring permanent satisfaction. أحمد accepts this description as literal الحق. He similarly affirms the الصراط, the bridge stretched over النار, and the reality of الجنة and النار as existing places prepared for their respective inhabitants. The dwellers of الجنة will enjoy endless bliss, and the greatest of their joys will be the vision of الله's face, which أحمد affirms against the Mu'tazilite denial of the beatific vision. The intercession (shafa'a) of النبي ﷺ is one of the most prominent articles in الأصول al-السنة. أحمد affirms that النبي ﷺ will intercede for the sinners of his الجماعة, that this intercession will be accepted by الله, and that people who deserved النار on account of their الذنوب will be brought out of it through النبي ﷺ's intercession and through divine الرحمة. This doctrine offers profound الأمل to المسلمون who are conscious of their own shortcomings and who الخوف divine العدل. أحمد أيضاً affirms that other الأنبياء, العلماء, and martyrs will intercede for those they are permitted to intercede for.
